questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If it rains, the match is cancelled. The match was not cancelled. Therefore,
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Modus tollens: deny the consequent to deny the antecedent.

**Working.** From 'rain → cancelled' and 'not cancelled', conclude 'not rain'.

**Answer.** It did not rain.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Statement: 'All safety helmets in the store are yellow.' Conclusion: 'No safety helmet in the store is blue.' Does the conclusion follow?
options: No, the conclusion does not follow
The conclusion is the opposite of the statement
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A thing of one exclusive colour cannot be another colour.

**Working.** If every helmet is yellow, none can be blue; the conclusion follows.

**Answer.** Yes, the conclusion follows.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All squares are rectangles. A given figure is not a rectangle. Therefore the figure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Contrapositive of 'square → rectangle' is 'not rectangle → not square'.

**Working.** Since it is not a rectangle, it cannot be a square.

**Answer.** is not a square.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a row of $20$ students, A is $7$th from the left. A's position from the right is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Position from right $=$ total $-$ position from left $+1$.

**Working.** $20-7+1=14$.

**Answer.** 14th.

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: The volcanic (fine-grained) equivalent of granite is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Granite (plutonic felsic) ↔ rhyolite (volcanic felsic).

**Working.**
Rhyolite.

**Answer.** Rhyolite

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: The metamorphic facies characterised by high pressure and low temperature is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Blueschist = high-P/low-T (subduction); granulite = high-T; hornfels = contact.

**Working.**
Blueschist.

**Answer.** Blueschist

questions.subject: Mathematics & Geostatistics
questions.stem: The population variance of $\{2, 7, 6\}$ is _____.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $90^\circ$ and dips $65^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=65^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $65^\circ$

questions.subject: Applied Geology · Remote Sensing & GIS
questions.stem: In a GIS, a continuously varying surface such as elevation is best stored as a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuous fields (DEMs) are efficiently stored as rasters.

**Working.**
Raster.

**Answer.** Raster

questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $150^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$
